div.p: State Colors 🟢 Green = Running / data available / success 🟣 Purple = Queue membership / synchronization 🔵 Blue = Condition check (diamond) ⚫ Dark = Sleeping (off CPU) 🔴 Red = Signal / error path 🟠 Orange = Thundering herd recovery Key Invariants • NEVER hold mydevice_mutex while calling wait_event_interruptible • ALWAYS recheck condition after waking (under mutex) • ALWAYS propagate -ERESTARTSYS without conversion • SET_INTERRUPTIBLE before RECHECK_INNER prevents missed wakeup
Skip sleep entirely Proceed to mutex acquire
Process enqueued in mydevice_read_queue DEFINE_WAIT(__wait) allocated
task->state = TASK_INTERRUPTIBLE In queue; wakeable by signal ⚠ Set BEFORE re-checking condition
CPU given to other tasks This process is SLEEPING task removed from runqueue Resumes after wake_up call
Off CPU entirely Waiting in mydevice_read_queue TASK_INTERRUPTIBLE
Writer called: wake_up_interruptible (&mydevice_read_queue) task->state = TASK_RUNNING Back on runqueue
Remove from wait queue task->state = TASK_RUNNING __wait entry freed
Data confirmed under mutex copy_to_user() executes buffer_data_len decremented memmove() shifts FIFO wake_up(write_queue) mutex_unlock()
finish_wait() called Removed from read_queue task->state = TASK_RUNNING
Signal received during sleep VFS layer sees -ERESTARTSYS → SA_RESTART: syscall restarted → no SA_RESTART: errno=EINTR
Another reader consumed data Mutex released without read Caller retries wait loop
Signal while waiting for mutex (Not while sleeping in wait queue) Same propagation rules apply
